body:
Hey Teresa, just wanted to confirm we're still on for the walkthrough Thursday morning at the Riverside Drive site. I'm planning to be there around 7:30 AM so we can get through the main areas before the crew arrives. Let me know if that time works or if we need to shift it at all.

Paul
snippet: Hey Teresa, just wanted to confirm we're still on for the walkthrough Thursday m...
subject: Job site walkthrough Thursday

body:
Hi Paul, Thursday at 7:30 works for me. I'll bring the updated site plans and the punch list from last week's review. If you can have the permit folder pulled before we go, that would be great — I want to make sure everything is squared away before the next inspection.

Teresa
snippet: Hi Paul, Thursday at 7:30 works for me. I'll bring the updated site plans and th...
subject: Re: Job site walkthrough Thursday

body:
Teresa, heads-up — the conduit and junction boxes are ready at Harrison Supply. I confirmed this morning they'll have everything staged by 6:30 AM. We're going to need at least two guys and the flatbed to get it all in one trip.

Paul
snippet: Teresa, heads-up — the conduit and junction boxes are ready at Harrison Supply. ...
subject: Material pickup tomorrow

body:
Got it, thanks for confirming. I'll put Marcus and Derrick on it — they can take the F-350 and the trailer. Can you make sure the PO number is on the pickup slip? Accounting flagged the last order for a missing reference number and I don't want another headache.

Teresa
snippet: Got it, thanks for confirming. I'll put Marcus and Derrick on it — they can take...
subject: Re: Material pickup tomorrow

body:
Already handled — gave Harrison the PO this morning and they're printing a packing slip with it attached. Marcus should be set. I'll text him the contact name there in case anything comes up on arrival.

Paul
snippet: Already handled — gave Harrison the PO this morning and they're printing a packi...
subject: Re: Material pickup tomorrow

body:
Hey, I finally got ahold of the building department. They have an opening Thursday at 10 AM for the rough-in inspection — that was the best slot this week. The next available after that isn't until Monday the 7th. Let me know if Thursday works, otherwise I'll push it.

Brian
snippet: Hey, I finally got ahold of the building department. They have an opening Thursd...
subject: Permit inspection scheduled

body:
Thursday at 10 should be fine. I have a meeting at 9 but it should wrap up by 9:30. Can you be on-site for it? I want to make sure someone who knows the work is there when the inspector walks through.

Teresa
snippet: Thursday at 10 should be fine. I have a meeting at 9 but it should wrap up by 9:...
subject: Re: Permit inspection scheduled

body:
I can be there — I'll head over right after drop-off. I'm also going to double-check the panel labeling tonight just to make sure everything looks clean before he comes through. Last thing I want is a correction notice.

Brian
snippet: I can be there — I'll head over right after drop-off. I'm also going to double-c...
subject: Re: Permit inspection scheduled

body:
Good call. Also make sure all the smoke detectors are in place — they flagged that on the Rodriguez job last month and it held things up for two weeks. I'll bring the permit folder Thursday morning in case they need to see the originals.

Teresa
snippet: Good call. Also make sure all the smoke detectors are in place — they flagged th...
subject: Re: Permit inspection scheduled

body:
Hey T, checking in from the site. The crew is making good progress on the second floor framing. Had a minor delay this morning — lumber delivery showed up about an hour late — but we're mostly back on track. Nothing major to flag.

Brian
snippet: Hey T, checking in from the site. The crew is making good progress on the second...
subject: Foreman check-in

body:
Glad it's moving. Was that the same supplier as the delay two weeks ago? If so I want to flag it with the project manager before it becomes a pattern. Also, did the concrete crew finish the pad yesterday or is that still outstanding?

Teresa
snippet: Glad it's moving. Was that the same supplier as the delay two weeks ago? If so I...
subject: Re: Foreman check-in

body:
Yeah, same supplier — Valley Lumber again. I'll note it in the daily log. The pad got finished yesterday afternoon just before 4 PM. Did a visual check this morning, no cracks or settling. Should be ready for framing on that section by end of week.

Brian
snippet: Yeah, same supplier — Valley Lumber again. I'll note it in the daily log. The pa...
subject: Re: Foreman check-in

body:
I'll send an email to the PM about Valley Lumber so we have it documented. Can you grab a couple photos of the pad and send them over? I want to keep a record in the project folder. Also, what's the headcount looking like tomorrow?

Teresa
snippet: I'll send an email to the PM about Valley Lumber so we have it documented. Can y...
subject: Re: Foreman check-in

body:
Sending two photos now, just took them. Tomorrow we've got six: me, Carlos, the two apprentices, and the Martinez brothers. Should be enough to push through the north wall section if the weather cooperates. Rain is supposed to clear by midmorning.

Brian
snippet: Sending two photos now, just took them. Tomorrow we've got six: me, Carlos, the ...
subject: Re: Foreman check-in

body:
Hey T! Are you free any day this week for lunch? Feel like we've barely talked outside the job site lately. There's that new taco place on Elm that opened last month — supposedly really good. Wednesday or Friday work best for me, but I'm flexible.

Danny
snippet: Hey T! Are you free any day this week for lunch? Feel like we've barely talked o...
subject: Lunch this week?

body:
Yes! Wednesday works perfectly — I have a light afternoon so no need to rush back. Let's do the taco place, I've been curious about it too. 12:30?

Teresa
snippet: Yes! Wednesday works perfectly — I have a light afternoon so no need to rush bac...
subject: Re: Lunch this week?

body:
Teresa, are you around Tuesday morning before work? I wanted to grab coffee and run something by you — nothing serious, just been thinking through some stuff and wanted your take. Stella's Café is right by the office, maybe 8 AM?

Danny
snippet: Teresa, are you around Tuesday morning before work? I wanted to grab coffee and ...
subject: Coffee next Tuesday?

body:
Tuesday works great! 8 AM at Stella's is perfect, I'm usually there early anyway. Now you've got me curious — everything okay? You can always text me if it's something that can't wait until then.

Teresa
snippet: Tuesday works great! 8 AM at Stella's is perfect, I'm usually there early anyway...
subject: Re: Coffee next Tuesday?

body:
Ha, all good I promise. Just thinking about whether to go for that lead position they posted and wanted to talk it through with someone I actually trust. See you Tuesday!

Danny
snippet: Ha, all good I promise. Just thinking about whether to go for that lead position...
subject: Re: Coffee next Tuesday?

body:
T!! Okay so Marcus's birthday is the 14th and I really want to do something fun this year. I was thinking brunch at The Perch — they do bottomless mimosas on weekends and the food is amazing. Are you in? Trying to nail down headcount before I call for a reservation.

Danny
snippet: T!! Okay so Marcus's birthday is the 14th and I really want to do something fun ...
subject: Birthday brunch plans

body:
I am absolutely in. Brian can watch the kids so I'm totally free that morning. The Perch is perfect — I went there for my sister's birthday last spring and it was so good. What time are you thinking, and does Marcus know or is this a surprise?

Teresa
snippet: I am absolutely in. Brian can watch the kids so I'm totally free that morning. T...
subject: Re: Birthday brunch plans

body:
He has no idea! I'm telling him we're doing something low-key at my place and then redirecting. Thinking 11 AM so we don't feel rushed. Can you get there a little early to help me stall — like 10:45?

Danny
snippet: He has no idea! I'm telling him we're doing something low-key at my place and th...
subject: Re: Birthday brunch plans

body:
10:45 I can do. I won't breathe a word, you know I love a good surprise. I already have a card for him. Should I grab a cake from that bakery on Grove or do you have dessert covered?

Teresa
snippet: 10:45 I can do. I won't breathe a word, you know I love a good surprise. I alrea...
subject: Re: Birthday brunch plans

body:
Hey, I got the renewal packet from the insurance company today for the home policy. Three tiers to choose from — rates went up about 8% across the board, which is annoying but not surprising. I can forward the PDF if you want to look it over together tonight.

Brian
snippet: Hey, I got the renewal packet from the insurance company today for the home poli...
subject: Renewal options

body:
Yes, please forward it. I don't want to auto-renew without checking the coverage differences. We added the fence and the HVAC unit last year and I want to make sure the replacement value reflects that.

Teresa
snippet: Yes, please forward it. I don't want to auto-renew without checking the coverage...
subject: Re: Renewal options

body:
Forwarded just now. I'm leaning toward the middle tier — the top one adds flood coverage but we're not in a flood zone, so it seems like overkill. The bottom tier dropped the equipment breakdown rider which I think we've actually used before.

Brian
snippet: Forwarded just now. I'm leaning toward the middle tier — the top one adds flood ...
subject: Re: Renewal options

body:
Looked it over and I agree on the middle tier. Can you call them tomorrow and ask about a loyalty discount? We've been with them six years. Also want to confirm the deductible — I thought it was $1,000 but the PDF is showing $1,500.

Teresa
snippet: Looked it over and I agree on the middle tier. Can you call them tomorrow and as...
subject: Re: Renewal options

body:
Just called — no loyalty discount unless we bundle with auto, which we don't have with them. And yes, the deductible went up to $1,500 starting this cycle, apparently standard across all policies in our state now. Still going with the middle tier?

Brian
snippet: Just called — no loyalty discount unless we bundle with auto, which we don't hav...
subject: Re: Renewal options

body:
Hey T, got an invoice from Mercer Supplies today and I don't recognize the PO number on it. It's for $847, dated the 18th. Did you order something from them recently? I don't want to pay it until we know we actually authorized it.

Brian
snippet: Hey T, got an invoice from Mercer Supplies today and I don't recognize the PO nu...
subject: Vendor invoice question

body:
Don't pay it yet. I did place an order with Mercer a few weeks back but it was only around $300 worth of fittings — that amount doesn't match. I'll pull our records tonight and call them first thing tomorrow to get a line-item breakdown before we do anything.

Teresa
snippet: Don't pay it yet. I did place an order with Mercer a few weeks back but it was o...
subject: Re: Vendor invoice question

body:
Hey, the lease renewal paperwork came from the landlord today. They're raising rent by $175 a month starting in September. The options are a 12-month or 24-month renewal. I think we should talk about whether this still makes sense given where we are with the house down payment savings.

Brian
snippet: Hey, the lease renewal paperwork came from the landlord today. They're raising r...
subject: Apartment lease renewal

body:
$175 is a lot — that's over 10% depending on what we're currently paying. I'd want to look at our savings timeline before committing to another year or two here. How long do we have to respond, and did they present this as a final number or is there room to negotiate?

Teresa
snippet: $175 is a lot — that's over 10% depending on what we're currently paying. I'd wa...
subject: Re: Apartment lease renewal

body:
We have until August 1st to respond. I already emailed the property manager asking about flexibility on the increase and she said she'd check with the owner. I'll keep you posted. Maybe this weekend we can sit down and look at the savings spreadsheet together?

Brian
snippet: We have until August 1st to respond. I already emailed the property manager aski...
subject: Re: Apartment lease renewal

body:
T, I know you've been slammed but I saved a couple of good pieces from my newsletters this week. One is about the new labor rules around contractor classifications — might be relevant to some of the stuff you've mentioned at work. The other is a solid longread on housing market trends in the northeast.

Brian
snippet: T, I know you've been slammed but I saved a couple of good pieces from my newsle...
subject: Newsletter — this week's reads

body:
Oh please send those links! The labor classification one especially — we've been dealing with some gray areas on that at work and I've been meaning to read up. What newsletter is the housing one from? I want to subscribe if it's a regular thing.

Teresa
snippet: Oh please send those links! The labor classification one especially — we've been...
subject: Re: Newsletter — this week's reads

body:
Sending both links now. The labor one is from Construction Dive — they do a solid weekly roundup. The housing one is from a Substack called The Lot Line, written by a real estate economist out of Boston. Really good, I've been following it for about six months.

Brian
snippet: Sending both links now. The labor one is from Construction Dive — they do a soli...
subject: Re: Newsletter — this week's reads

body:
Just subscribed to The Lot Line — the archive alone looks worth digging into. Skimmed the labor article too and it's exactly what I needed. Going to read it properly tonight. Thanks for passing these along, you always find the good stuff.

Teresa
snippet: Just subscribed to The Lot Line — the archive alone looks worth digging into. Sk...
subject: Re: Newsletter — this week's reads

body:
Hey, wanted to nail down pickup for this week. I can cover Monday, Wednesday, and Friday if you take Tuesday and Thursday — but let me know if your schedule changed. I remember you mentioned a possible late meeting Thursday.

Brian
snippet: Hey, wanted to nail down pickup for this week. I can cover Monday, Wednesday, an...
subject: Schedule check — kids pickup

body:
Thursday I definitely need you to cover — that meeting is confirmed, probably until 5:30 at the earliest. Tuesday I can do. Your plan for the rest of the week works. Also, can you grab Sofia's art project from her classroom Thursday? Her teacher sent a note that it's ready.

Teresa
snippet: Thursday I definitely need you to cover — that meeting is confirmed, probably un...
subject: Re: Schedule check — kids pickup

body:
Got it, I'll pick up Sofia's project Thursday. So to confirm: you have Tuesday, I have Monday, Wednesday, Thursday, Friday. Quick question — is Miguel's soccer practice still 4:30 on Wednesday or did they move it? Coach Reyes sent something but I missed it.

Brian
snippet: Got it, I'll pick up Sofia's project Thursday. So to confirm: you have Tuesday, ...
subject: Re: Schedule check — kids pickup

body:
Practice is still 4:30 but they moved to the east fields, not the main turf. I'll forward Coach Reyes's email so you have the address. That schedule looks right to me — I'll pack their bags tonight so everything's ready Monday morning.

Teresa
snippet: Practice is still 4:30 but they moved to the east fields, not the main turf. I'l...
subject: Re: Schedule check — kids pickup

body:
Got the forward, thanks — east fields noted. One more thing: Grandma Rosa called and asked if the kids could stay with her Friday night. I told her I'd check with you first. Could be a nice break for us if the timing works. Thoughts?

Brian
snippet: Got the forward, thanks — east fields noted. One more thing: Grandma Rosa called...
subject: Re: Schedule check — kids pickup

body:
Hey T, I started pulling together some options for the August trip. I'm looking at a lake cabin in Vermont or a beach rental in Maine — both are in budget range. Vermont would be a bit cheaper but Maine has more activities for the kids. Want me to send both links so we can compare?

Brian
snippet: Hey T, I started pulling together some options for the August trip. I'm looking ...
subject: Family vacation planning

body:
Yes, send both! My gut says Maine since the kids have been asking about the beach all summer. But let's check the dates carefully — I need to make sure we don't overlap with the project deadline I have in mid-August. The week of the 4th should be clear if we go then.

Teresa
snippet: Yes, send both! My gut says Maine since the kids have been asking about the beac...
subject: Re: Family vacation planning

body:
Hey, any thoughts on the weekend? I was thinking something low-key Saturday — farmers market in the morning and then let the kids run around at the park. Sunday I might need a couple hours to finish up some stuff around the house but I'm free after noon.

Brian
snippet: Hey, any thoughts on the weekend? I was thinking something low-key Saturday — fa...
subject: Weekend plans

body:
That sounds really good actually, I could use a relaxed weekend. Farmers market Saturday morning works — we're almost out of coffee and I want to grab some from that roaster we liked last time. Sunday afternoon walk sounds nice too if the weather cooperates.

Teresa
snippet: That sounds really good actually, I could use a relaxed weekend. Farmers market ...
subject: Re: Weekend plans

body:
Perfect. I'll try to leave early Saturday before it gets too crowded — the kids do so much better when it's not packed. Forecast looks decent for Sunday so let's plan on the walk. Also, do you want to see if Danny wants to join us Saturday? She mentioned wanting to get out of the house this weekend.

Brian
snippet: Perfect. I'll try to leave early Saturday before it gets too crowded — the kids ...
subject: Re: Weekend plans
